rationale: The cited paper appears in the empirical methods section, where the authors justify using synthetic difference-in-differences to handle preperiod differences in their store-level data. They cite Keller, Guyt, and Grewal (2024) as a marketing-domain precedent for this identification approach, alongside the original method source (Arkhangelsky et al. 2021). This gives the citation a clear functional role in analysis design, even though the soda-tax substantive findings are not used.
relation_description: The citing paper applies a methodological approach that the cited paper also uses, referencing it as precedent for synthetic difference-in-differences to construct a credible counterfactual in observational retail data. The link is methodological transfer, not theoretical extension of soda-tax results.
